title: Covellia glomerata
intro: Covellia glomerata, also known as Scurvy Grass or Covell's grass, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to treat scurvy due to its high vitamin C content, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Covellia glomerata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Covellia glomerata include teas and infusions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Covellia glomerata is a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The classification of this plant is further categorized under the genus Covellia and the subfamily Ruschioideae. Covellia glomerata is part of the order Caryophyllales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
